rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Contest Settlement And Application For Order Thereon, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANTS MARGARET POPPIN, SNYDER TRUST ENTERPRISES, JOHN POPPIN, 706 SANSOME PROPERTIES Motion To Contest Settlement and Application for Order Thereon. Motion granted. The June 10, 2005 Stipulation and Order is not a settlement implicating CCP section 877.6 because Fuchs and Snyder are not co-obligors or joint tortfeasors. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DELOIS VS. BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT BARRETT BLOCK PARTNERS Motion for Judgment on The Pleadings. Motion granted without leave to amend as to first cause of action based upon CCP section 1061. Motion granted without leave to amend as to 7th and 9th causes of action as conceded. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 6, PLAINTIFF GIANCARLO MARANGHI MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication denied. Notice of motion and motion do not affirmatively dispose of an entire cause of action or affirmative defense under CCP section 437c(f)(1) or CRC 3.1350. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 11, PETITIONER NORTHERN CALIFORNIA CARPENTERS REGIONAL COUNCIL Hearing On Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board Of California'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Compliance With Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 14, Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board of California's Order to Show Cause Regarding Compliance with Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10). Is denied. The subpoenas are overbroad, seeking all records dating back to Janaury 1, 2008 although the suspect prescriptions began in January 2009. Insufficient foundation for Dr. Miller's opinions. Respondent's doctors explain questioned dosages are sometimes proper. (Wood vs. Superior Court of Sacramento County (1985) 166 Cal.App. 3d 1138.) =(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANT WAYNE FULLER DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot. On January 19, 2010, the Court granted Plaintiff leave to file a second Amended Complaint. =(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 12, DEFENDANT WAYNE FULLER MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot. On January 19, 2010, the Court granted Plaintiff leave to file a second Amended Complaint. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMANCE MORTGAGE CERTIFICATE FUND, LLC A VS. DEBRA HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-495306 WITH CASE CUD-09-631022
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 24, 2010, line 15, PLAINTIFF PERFORMANCE MORTGAGE CERTIFICATE FUND, LLC A MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-495306 WITH CASE CUD-09-631022. Is denied because (1) Motion is moot because plaintiff obtained judgment against defendant on 2/1/2010; (2) Motion was not timely served; and (3) Motion lacks any evidentiary support. =(302/CWW)
